executiveSummary.summary: The core concept is sound and addresses a real pain point, but you're entering a market dominated by well-funded players with massive head starts. Your education background is valuable, but $10K and solo execution against Khan Academy's Khanmigo creates significant execution risk. The B2C path offers more control but harder customer acquisition.

marketSize.sam: $2.1B online tutoring segment for grades 6-12 math
marketSize.som: $500K-1M realistic year 1-2 obtainable market (0.02-0.05% of SAM)
marketSize.methodology: Based on 50M US students grades 6-12, 15% needing math help, 5% willing to pay for AI tutoring at $29/mo

competitors.name: Khan Academy (Khanmigo)
competitors.description: Free platform with AI tutoring feature, 120M users
competitors.strength: Massive brand trust, free core content, GPT-4 powered

competitors.name: Photomath
competitors.weakness: Doesn't teach underlying concepts or methodology

competitors.name: Varsity Tutors
competitors.weakness: Higher price point, less personalized AI

marketAnalysis.positioning: The conversational math tutor that thinks like a great teacher
marketAnalysis.timing: Good - AI tutoring acceptance growing, but Khan Academy already captured early market

valueProposition.coreProblem: Middle-class families can't afford $50-100/hr human tutors but need more than generic video content
valueProposition.targetCustomer: Parents of grades 6-12 students struggling with math, household income $50K-$150K, value education highly
valueProposition.uniqueAngle: Socratic dialogue approach that mirrors how great teachers actually teach - through questions, not answers
valueProposition.whyNow: LLMs finally enable natural conversation, parents more comfortable with AI post-ChatGPT adoption

businessModel.revenueStrategy: Subscription SaaS - $29/mo individual, $49/mo family (up to 3 kids)
businessModel.pricingFramework: $29/mo = cost of 20 minutes with human tutor, positioned as premium vs free alternatives
businessModel.scalabilityAssessment: High marginal scalability once core AI is built, but customer acquisition cost likely increases with competition

technicalFeasibility.recommendedStack: Next.js frontend, Node.js backend, OpenAI API, PostgreSQL, Stripe payments

buildVsBuy.rationale: OpenAI API much cheaper than training custom model

technicalRisks: OpenAI API costs scaling with usage
Prompt engineering for consistent Socratic method

risks.description: Khan Academy could improve Khanmigo's Socratic capabilities and maintain free pricing
risks.mitigation: Focus on superior pedagogy and parent reporting that Khan Academy neglects

risks.description: Parents may resist AI tutoring due to screen time concerns or preference for human interaction
risks.mitigation: Position as supplement to human teaching, emphasize learning outcomes over screen time

risks.description: $10K insufficient for 6+ months of development plus customer acquisition
risks.mitigation: Seek pre-orders, consider part-time development, or find technical co-founder

risks.description: Schools may ban AI tutoring tools or require specific compliance
risks.mitigation: Start B2C to avoid school procurement complexity

risks.description: AI may give incorrect mathematical guidance or fail to maintain Socratic approach
risks.mitigation: Extensive testing, human review of AI responses, clear disclaimers

week1to4: Validate demand: survey 100 parents about AI tutoring willingness
Analyze Khanmigo's current capabilities and gaps
Build basic Socratic dialogue prototype for linear equations
week5to8: Refine Socratic prompting based on feedback
week9to12: Build MVP for Algebra I topics
Set up basic payment and user management
Create landing page and start email list

decisionAgenda.question: Should you focus on algebra-only or attempt grades 6-12 math?

options.tradeoff: Deeper quality and faster launch vs smaller addressable market
Larger market vs diluted focus and longer development

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Start with Algebra I only. Your $10K budget and solo status require extreme focus. You can expand after proving the concept works.

decisionAgenda.question: B2C (parents) or B2B (schools) go-to-market?

options.tradeoff: Faster iteration and feedback vs higher customer acquisition cost
Larger deal sizes vs long sales cycles and compliance requirements

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Start B2C. Schools have 12-18 month procurement cycles you can't afford to wait for. Parents can give immediate feedback on your Socratic approach.

decisionAgenda.question: Should you seek a technical co-founder or stay solo?

options.tradeoff: Faster development and shared equity vs finding right person and equity dilution
Full control and ownership vs slower development and higher risk

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Stay solo for now but actively network for potential co-founder. Your teaching background is the key asset - don't dilute it too early.
